steps.name: Define Requirements
steps.description: Clarify functional and non-functional requirements. Ask about scale, users, read/write ratio.

steps.name: Resource/API Design
steps.description: Define core API endpoints and data models.
tips: Define key entities and their relationships
Think about pagination for list endpoints

steps.name: Scale Architecture
steps.description: Draw high-level architecture. Deep dive into 2-3 critical components.
tips: Start with simple design, then scale
Add caching, queues, CDN as needed

steps.name: Confirm Trade-offs
steps.description: Walk through requirements, discuss trade-offs and alternatives.

steps.name: Clarify Requirements
steps.description: Ask about scope, constraints, and key behaviors. Identify actors and use cases.

steps.name: Identify Core Entities
steps.description: List the main objects/classes that will exist in the system.

steps.name: Define Interfaces/APIs
steps.description: Define the public methods and interfaces before implementation.

steps.name: Design Class Hierarchy
steps.description: Establish inheritance, composition, and relationships between classes.
tips: Use UML if it helps communicate

steps.name: Implement Core Logic
steps.description: Write the key algorithms and business logic.

steps.name: Discuss Trade-offs
steps.description: Explain design decisions, alternatives considered, and extensibility.
tips: Suggest what you'd add with more time

hld: Add message queue for async processing
Walk through a complete request flow

antiPatterns.name: Jumping to Solution
antiPatterns.description: Starting with architecture before understanding requirements.
antiPatterns.fix: Always spend 5 minutes on requirements first. Repeat them back to the interviewer.

antiPatterns.name: Over-Engineering
antiPatterns.description: Adding unnecessary complexity like microservices for a simple problem.
antiPatterns.fix: Start simple. Add complexity only when the scale demands it.

antiPatterns.name: Ignoring Scale
antiPatterns.description: Designing for 100 users when the problem says 100M.
antiPatterns.fix: Always do back-of-envelope estimation. Let numbers drive design decisions.

antiPatterns.name: Monologue Mode
antiPatterns.description: Talking for 10+ minutes without checking in with the interviewer.
antiPatterns.fix: Pause every 3-5 minutes. Ask 'Does this make sense?' or 'Should I dive deeper here?'

antiPatterns.name: No Trade-offs
antiPatterns.description: Presenting one solution as if it's perfect without discussing alternatives.
antiPatterns.fix: Every design choice is a trade-off. State what you're gaining and what you're giving up.

antiPatterns.name: God Class
antiPatterns.description: Putting all logic in one massive class.
antiPatterns.fix: Apply SRP. If a class has more than one reason to change, split it.

antiPatterns.name: Premature Pattern Application
antiPatterns.description: Forcing design patterns where they don't naturally fit.
antiPatterns.fix: Let patterns emerge from the problem. Don't use Strategy when a simple if/else suffices.
